phase1: Map the public API surface: all exported symbols in __init__.py — assess naming and stability
Review type hint coverage: are all public function signatures fully annotated?
Check docstring completeness: module, class, and public method docstrings (Google/NumPy style)
Assess backwards compatibility risk: any changes to public API signatures or behavior?
Document CLI entry points (typer, click, argparse) and their argument validation
Check packaging metadata: pyproject.toml completeness, version constraints, classifiers
phase3: Annotate all public functions and methods with complete type hints; use typing.Protocol for duck-typed interfaces
Keep public API minimal and stable; mark experimental APIs with warnings.warn(DeprecationWarning)
Provide Google-style docstrings with Args, Returns, Raises, and Examples sections on all public symbols
Write pure functions where possible — minimize side effects in library code
Export only the intended public interface from __init__.py; use __all__ to enforce it
Design CLI commands to be thin wrappers around library functions for testability
phase4: Test public API functions directly — test the interface, not the implementation
Use pytest.mark.parametrize for input boundary testing
Test type correctness with mypy in strict mode as part of the test suite
Test CLI commands with click.testing.CliRunner or typer.testing.CliRunner
Write doctest examples in docstrings; run them with pytest --doctest-modules
